slides.title: All About Me and My Family
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 3 English, Unit 1: All About Me and My Family. Welcome to your first unit of Year 3 English! In this unit you will learn how to talk about yourself and your family, and about the good values that hold a family and a school together — respect, honour, and duty. You will learn how to behave well in public and in private, and how to talk about health and safety. By the end, you will be ready to introduce your family and share what matters to you, just like Musa does in this unit's story. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Talk about the people in your family and what you do together
Use new words like respect, honour, duty, health, and safety
Say what is happening now, using the present continuous (“I am helping.”)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to talk about the people in your family and what you do together. you will also use new words like respect, honour, duty, health, and safety. you will also say what is happening now, using the present continuous (“i am helping.”). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: family, parent, friend, student, junior, senior. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes My Family and Good Behaviour at School. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Present Continuous  (what is happening now)
bullets: We use the present continuous to talk about actions that are happening right now. It helps us say exactly what people are doing at this moment, like “The...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Present Continuous  (what is happening now). We use the present continuous to talk about actions that are happening right now. It helps us say exactly what people are doing at this moment, like “The student is showing respect.” Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 — Introduce Your Family. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
